title: Monolithic vs Multi-Agent
concept: A decision guide for when to transition a monolithic agent to a multi-agent system based on task complexity.

exercises.prompt: When working with multiple AI agents, which is important to ensure smooth operation?
exercises.explain: Specialized roles in a multi-agent system help each agent perform its best in complex tasks.

cards.title: Monolithic vs Multi-Agent
cards.body: Choosing between a single-agent (monolithic) or multiple agents (multi-agent) setup is key for tackling tasks effectively.

cards.title: What is Monolithic?
cards.body: A monolithic system uses one single agent to perform all tasks, suitable for simple problems.

cards.title: Building Analogy
cards.body: A monolithic agent is like a solo builder constructing a house alone, while a multi-agent system resembles a team of builders collaborating on different aspects of construction.

cards.title: Real-World Example
cards.body: A monolithic agent might handle basic reminders while a multi-agent system can manage a whole event with scheduling, invitations, and logistics.

cards.title: Myth: More Agents Equals Better Performance
cards.body: It's a myth that adding more agents always improves performance; it depends on the task complexity.

cards.title: Task Complexity
cards.body: Understanding when to switch from monolithic to multi-agent is crucial based on the task's needs.

right.text: Multiple agents specializing in different tasks

exercises.prompt: When should you consider using a multi-agent system?

options.text: When tasks are complex and require specialization
Only if you have lots of agents available
If you prefer a single solution

exercises.explain: A multi-agent system is ideal for complex tasks that benefit from the specialization of multiple agents.

exercises.prompt: A monolithic agent is best for handling complex tasks exclusively.
exercises.explain: Monolithic agents are designed for simpler tasks, not for complex situations where specialization is needed.

exercises.prompt: You should switch to a multi-agent system when the task becomes too ______.
exercises.sentence: You should switch to a multi-agent system when the task becomes too complex.
exercises.explain: The blank should be filled with 'complex' as that signifies when a multi-agent system is needed.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario best illustrates the transition from a monolithic to a multi-agent system?

options.text: An AI reminder app for personal tasks
An AI team coordinating a conference
A single agent solving math problems

exercises.explain: A team coordinating a conference involves various tasks that require multiple specialized agents to operate efficiently.

exercises.prompt: What factors should you consider when deciding whether to use a monolithic or multi-agent system?

options.text: Task complexity and need for specialization
Duration of task and agent downtime

exercises.explain: Task complexity and the need for specialization are vital in determining whether to stick with a monolithic agent or transition to a multi-agent system.
